title: Late night on the train
creator: Kinzy, Quentin
description: Looking down from the upper floor of a Metra Gallery Car.
image_alt_text: Railing running diagonally, with green-tinted train windows behind them.
subject: Railroad trains; Transportation

title: Climbing Monks Mound
creator: Kinzy, Quentin
description: Monks Mound (or Mound 38) is the largest pre-Columbian earthwork in North America. It was built in the city of Cahokia, which once stood near modern-day St. Louis as well as the confluence of the Mississippi and Missouri rivers. This was captured standing at the foot of Monks Mound to offer a sense of its scale.
image_alt_text: A grassy earthen mound, pyramid-shaped with two terraces visible. A stairway runs up the center of the mound, with two people walking down it, who look small in scale compared to the mound.
location: Cahokia Mounds State Historic Park (Collinsville, Illinois)
subject: Mississippi River Valley; History; Architecture

title: October flowers
creator: Kinzy, Quentin
description: This photo of flowers in a small, local park is the last image on its roll of film. I was taking a couple photos at this small, local park to use up the last of my film, and I wasn’t even sure whether this image would fit. The right side of the frame is the product of light exposure, which I have intentionally left uncropped.
image_alt_text: Upward view of flowers against a clear sky. The colors are muted, slightly yellowed as if aged. A blank white strip runs along the right edge.
subject: Flowers; Silhouettes; Plants
genre: art photography; 35mm (photographic film size); nature photography

title: Autumn prairie
creator: Kinzy, Quentin
description: Switchgrass (light yellow, at front) growing in a restored prairie in Iowa.
image_alt_text: Light and dark yellow prairie grasses and a few sparsely covered trees under a blue sky.
location: Cedar River Crossing Conservation Area (Solon, Iowa)
subject: Prairies; Switchgrass; Plants; Nature conservation
genre: art photography; 35mm (photographic film size); nature photography

title: Red flag advisory
creator: Kinzy, Quentin
description: This red flag serves as a warning to beachgoers that it is unsafe to go in the water due to hazards like riptides.
image_alt_text: A red flag waves in the wind close to the shoreline, ocean waves breaking in the background. The flag has an icon of a swimmer crossed out, but it is partially obscured by the ripples in the flag.
subject: Ocean; Beaches; Signs and signboards
genre: art photography; 35mm (photographic film size); travel photography

title: Coral conservation
creator: Kinzy, Quentin
description: These coral species are part of the Florida Reef Tract Rescue Project, a nationwide initiative to rescue and conserve Floridian coral in response to the outbreak of stony coral tissue loss disease.
image_alt_text: Several species of coral sit in a large tank under a purple grow light.
location: National Mississippi River Museum and Aquarium (Dubuque, Iowa)
subject: Corals; Animals; Nature conservation

title: Live music nightly
creator: Kinzy, Quentin
description: Point-and-shoot film cameras have very limited options for adjustments, compared to other film cameras. This camera had a fixed focus, film speed, and shutter speed. Essentially, my only decision was whether to cover the built-in, automatic flash with my thumb or not. I was very uncertain whether this photo would come out and feel very grateful that it did.
image_alt_text: A thin, blue neon sign in a window, with the text "Live music nightly."
subject: Neon signs; Signs and signboards
genre: art photography; night photography; 35mm (photographic film size); travel photography

title: Confluence

title: Steamboat
creator: Kinzy, Quentin
description: This steamboat, the William M. Black, originally dredged rivers including the Mississippi and Missouri. It now floats in a harbor of the Mississippi River, as part of the National Mississippi River Museum and Aquarium.
image_alt_text: A steamboat floating in a frozen harbor, with the text "William M. Black" across its side.
location: National Mississippi River Museum and Aquarium (Dubuque, Iowa)
subject: Boats and boating; Transportation; History; Museums; Mississippi River Valley; Missouri River Valley
